Department.GetByCode: SELECT * FROM Department WHERE DepartmentCode COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci = @DepartmentCode COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci
Position.GetByCode: SELECT * FROM Position WHERE PositionCode COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci = @PositionCode COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci
Employee.GetByCode: SELECT * FROM Employee WHERE EmployeeCode COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci = @EmployeeCode COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci
Employee.GetByDepartmentId: SELECT * FROM Employee WHERE DepartmentID=@DepartmentID AND IsDeleted = FALSE
Employee.CountByDepartmentId: SELECT COUNT(1) FROM Employee WHERE DepartmentID=@DepartmentID AND IsDeleted = FALSE
Employee.GetByPositionId: SELECT * FROM Employee WHERE PositionID=@PositionID AND IsDeleted = FALSE
Employee.GetByFilter: SELECT * FROM Employee WHERE IsDeleted=FALSE AND (@DepartmentID IS NULL OR DepartmentID=@DepartmentID) AND (@PositionID IS NULL OR PositionID=@PositionID) AND (@salaryFrom IS NULL OR Salary>=@salaryFrom) AND (@salaryTo IS NULL OR Salary<=@salaryTo) AND (@gender IS NULL OR Gender=@gender) AND (@hireDateFrom IS NULL OR HireDate>=DATE(@hireDateFrom)) AND (@hireDateTo IS NULL OR HireDate<=DATE(@hireDateTo)) ORDER BY HireDate IS NULL, HireDate DESC, CreatedDate DESC, EmployeeID LIMIT @pageSize OFFSET @offset
Employee.GetByFilterCount: SELECT COUNT(1) FROM Employee WHERE IsDeleted=FALSE AND (@DepartmentID IS NULL OR DepartmentID=@DepartmentID) AND (@PositionID IS NULL OR PositionID=@PositionID) AND (@salaryFrom IS NULL OR Salary>=@salaryFrom) AND (@salaryTo IS NULL OR Salary<=@salaryTo) AND (@gender IS NULL OR Gender=@gender) AND (@hireDateFrom IS NULL OR HireDate>=DATE(@hireDateFrom)) AND (@hireDateTo IS NULL OR HireDate<=DATE(@hireDateTo))
